title: Damage & Combat
subtitle: How damage, elements, resistances, and progression work in Tap of Exile.
elemSub: 5 damage elements with distinct mechanics and resistance interactions.
physicalDesc: Default damage type. Reduced by armor (physical resistance).
fireDesc: Elemental fire damage. Reduced by fire resistance.
lightningDesc: Elemental lightning damage. Reduced by lightning resistance.
coldDesc: Elemental cold damage. Reduced by cold resistance.
pureDesc: Ignores ALL resistances. Cannot be mitigated.
calcDesc: Your total damage is split across elements based on your damage profile. Each element's damage is reduced by the target's corresponding resistance. Pure damage ignores all resistances entirely. You always deal at least 1 damage per hit.
defaultProfileDesc: All characters start dealing 100% physical damage. You can add elemental splits (fire, lightning, cold) through the passive skill tree to exploit enemy weaknesses.
scalingSub: How monster stats scale with act, order, and rarity.
scalingDesc: Monster HP, damage, gold, and XP all grow as you progress deeper into each act and into higher acts. Rarer monsters are significantly tougher but reward more gold and experience. Each new act dramatically increases monster power — Act 5 enemies are many times stronger than Act 1.
scalingVariance: HP and damage also have a small random variance, so encounters feel slightly different each time.
playerResist1: Base resistances come from class selection
playerResist2: Additional resistance from skill tree nodes
playerResist4: Higher resistance means less damage taken from that element
monsterResist2: Higher rarity monsters gain bonus resistance
levelCapDesc: XP required per level grows progressively. Fighting enemies far above or below your level yields reduced XP — aim for enemies close to your level for optimal progression.
critDesc: All classes start with a 5% crit chance and 150% crit multiplier. Improve these through equipment, passive tree, and class abilities.
activeSub: Each class has 8 unique skills — damage abilities, buffs, debuffs, and heals. Stronger skills have longer cooldowns.
timingDesc: Enemies attack at a steady rhythm during combat. There is a short delay when a new enemy spawns before they begin attacking. Staying AFK for too long will cause attacks to stack up, so stay engaged!
